summary: SECTR, an American autonomous anti-UAV system using AI-powered acoustic tracking, has been unveiled by Talon Avionics, operating passively via microphone arrays to detect propeller signatures. Ukrainian forces are employing camouflage techniques for radar stations like the RPS-42 and ELR 55307, key components of their short-range air defense, as detailed in leaked operational manuals. The Rubicon Center has documented over 23,000 enemy target strikes, with 36.4% being unmanned systems, and recently reported 127 targets destroyed in a single day across multiple fronts, including Donbas and Belgorod. Meanwhile, the U.S.-supplied Hornet UAV, noted for its low noise, speed exceeding 200 km/h, and resistance to electronic warfare, has been observed in use. Ukrainian forces continue to leverage FPV drones effectively, including strikes on Russian soldiers using motorcycles and in ambush scenarios, while also countering Russian FPV attacks with electronic warfare and drone detection systems.
